Apple___Apple_scab.description: A fungal disease caused by Venturia inaequalis that affects leaves and fruit, especially in cool, wet spring weather.
Apple___Apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to brown velvety spots on leaves; dark, scabby, corky lesions on fruit; premature leaf drop.
Apple___Apple_scab.treatment: Apply fungicides (captan or myclobutanil) starting at bud break and repeat per label intervals; remove and destroy fallen leaves.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Plant scab-resistant cultivars; prune for good air circulation; rake and dispose of fallen leaves each autumn.

Apple___Black_rot.description: A fungal disease caused by Botryosphaeria obtusa affecting leaves, fruit, and bark.
Apple___Black_rot.symptoms: Purple-bordered spots on leaves that enlarge with concentric rings (frogeye); rotted fruit with concentric brown/black rings; cankers on branches.
Apple___Black_rot.treatment: Prune out cankers and dead wood well below visible infection; apply fungicide sprays during the growing season.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Remove mummified fruit and dead wood from the orchard; maintain tree vigor with proper fertilization and watering.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.description: A fungal disease (Gymnosporangium juniperi-virginianae) that requires both apple and cedar/juniper hosts to complete its life cycle.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.symptoms: Bright orange-yellow spots on leaves; small tube-like structures on the underside of leaves; lesions on fruit.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.treatment: Apply a protective fungicide (e.g., myclobutanil) starting in early spring; remove infected leaves where practical.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Plant rust-resistant apple varieties; avoid planting apple trees near junipers/cedars when possible.

Apple___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Apple___healthy.prevention: Maintain regular watering, balanced fertilization, and routine monitoring for early signs of pests or disease.

Blueberry___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper soil pH (4.5-5.5), consistent watering, and mulching to keep plants vigorous.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.description: A fungal disease caused by Podosphaera clandestina that thrives in warm, humid conditions.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery fungal growth on leaves and shoots; leaf curling, distortion, and stunted growth.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur-based or other labeled fungicides at first sign of disease; prune to improve airflow.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Avoid excess nitrogen fertilization; space and prune trees for good air circulation.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Maintain regular pruning, watering, and monitoring for early signs of pests or disease.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.description: A fungal disease caused by Cercospora zeae-maydis, favored by warm, humid weather.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.symptoms: Rectangular, gray to tan lesions that run parallel to the leaf veins.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.treatment: Apply foliar fungicides when disease pressure is high; rotate crops to reduce inoculum.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Use resistant hybrids; practice crop rotation and manage crop residue.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.description: A fungal disease caused by Puccinia sorghi.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.symptoms: Small, circular to elongate, golden-brown to cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.treatment: Apply fungicides if infection is severe and occurs early in the season; usually not economically necessary otherwise.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids; monitor fields early in the growing season.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.description: A fungal disease caused by Exserohilum turcicum.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.symptoms: Long, cigar-shaped, gray-green to tan lesions on leaves that can merge and cause extensive blighting.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.treatment: Apply fungicides at early disease onset; rotate crops to reduce residue-borne inoculum.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Use resistant hybrids; till under or manage crop residue after harvest.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper fertilization, crop rotation, and routine field monitoring.

Grape___Black_rot.description: A fungal disease caused by Guignardia bidwellii that affects leaves, shoots, and fruit.
Grape___Black_rot.symptoms: Reddish-brown circular spots on leaves; berries shrivel into hard black 'mummies'.
Grape___Black_rot.treatment: Apply fungicides starting early in the season through fruit set; remove and destroy mummified berries.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune for good canopy airflow; remove infected plant debris and mummies from the vineyard floor.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).description: A fungal trunk disease complex affecting the vascular system of grapevines.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).symptoms: 'Tiger-stripe' interveinal discoloration on leaves; dark spotted ('measled') berries; internal wood decay.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).treatment: No effective cure exists; remove and destroy severely infected vines, or attempt trunk renewal in early stages.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Avoid pruning during wet weather; protect pruning wounds with a sealant to prevent fungal entry.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.description: A fungal disease caused by Pseudocercospora vitis (Isariopsis), favored by warm, humid conditions.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.symptoms: Dark, angular spots on leaves that enlarge and merge, leading to premature defoliation.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.treatment: Apply fungicide sprays during the growing season; remove and destroy heavily infected leaves.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Improve canopy ventilation through pruning; avoid overhead irrigation that wets foliage.

Grape___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Grape___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper canopy management, irrigation, and routine vineyard monitoring.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).description: A serious bacterial disease (Candidatus Liberibacter spp.) spread by the Asian citrus psyllid; currently incurable.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).symptoms: Blotchy, mottled yellowing of leaves; yellow shoots; lopsided, bitter, partially green fruit; premature fruit drop.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).treatment: No cure exists; remove and destroy infected trees promptly to limit spread; control the psyllid vector with insecticides.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free planting stock; monitor for and control Asian citrus psyllid populations.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas arboricola pv. pruni.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked lesions on leaves that turn dark and may drop out leaving a 'shot-hole' look; sunken lesions on fruit.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides during the dormant and growing season per label guidance; avoid overhead irrigation.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant resistant varieties where available; avoid working in orchards when foliage is wet.

Peach___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Peach___healthy.prevention: Maintain regular pruning, watering, and monitoring for early signs of pests or disease.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas campestris pv. vesicatoria.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown with yellow halos; raised, scabby lesions on fruit.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides; remove and destroy infected plant debris.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed and transplants; rotate crops; avoid overhead watering.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper watering, fertilization, and routine monitoring.

Potato___Early_blight.description: A fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani, typically appearing on older, lower leaves first.
Potato___Early_blight.symptoms: Dark, concentric 'target-spot' lesions on older leaves, often surrounded by a yellow halo.
Potato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides at first symptoms; remove and destroy heavily infected foliage.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops; ensure adequate plant nutrition; avoid overhead watering late in the day.

Potato___Late_blight.description: Caused by the oomycete Phytophthora infestans, the pathogen responsible for the Irish potato famine. Spreads rapidly in cool, wet weather.
Potato___Late_blight.symptoms: Water-soaked, pale to dark green lesions on leaves that rapidly turn brown/black; white fuzzy fungal growth on undersides in humid conditions.
Potato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides promptly at first sign of disease; remove and destroy infected plants to slow spread.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties; ensure good drainage and airflow; avoid overhead irrigation.

Potato___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Potato___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper crop rotation, watering, and routine field monitoring.

Raspberry___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper pruning, watering, and routine monitoring.

Soybean___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Soybean___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper crop rotation, fertilization, and routine field monitoring.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.description: A fungal disease caused by Podosphaera xanthii or Erysiphe cichoracearum.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.symptoms: White, powdery patches on leaves and stems; leaf yellowing, curling, and early senescence.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate-based fungicides; remove heavily infected leaves.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Provide adequate plant spacing for airflow; plant powdery-mildew-resistant varieties.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.description: A fungal disease caused by Diplocarpon earlianum.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.symptoms: Small, irregular purplish spots that merge into larger blotches, giving leaves a scorched appearance.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.treatment: Apply fungicides at first sign of disease; remove infected leaves after harvest (renovation).
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use resistant varieties; avoid overhead irrigation; renovate beds annually after harvest.

Strawberry___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper watering, mulching, and routine monitoring.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.description: A bacterial disease caused by Xanthomonas spp.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit, often with yellow halos.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.treatment: Apply copper-based bactericides; remove and destroy infected plant debris.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use certified disease-free seed and transplants; avoid overhead irrigation; rotate crops.

Tomato___Early_blight.description: A fungal disease caused by Alternaria solani.
Tomato___Early_blight.symptoms: Concentric 'target-spot' lesions on older leaves; can also affect stems and fruit.
Tomato___Early_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides at first symptoms; remove infected lower leaves.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops; mulch to reduce soil splash onto leaves; stake or cage plants for better airflow.

Tomato___Late_blight.description: Caused by the oomycete Phytophthora infestans; can destroy a crop within days under favorable conditions.
Tomato___Late_blight.symptoms: Large, water-soaked, gray-green lesions on leaves, often with white mold visible on the undersides.
Tomato___Late_blight.treatment: Apply fungicides immediately at first sign of disease; remove and destroy infected plants.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Use resistant varieties; avoid overhead watering; ensure good air circulation between plants.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.description: A fungal disease caused by Passalora fulva (syn. Fulvia fulva), common in humid greenhouse conditions.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.symptoms: Pale green to yellow spots on the upper leaf surface; olive-green velvety mold growth on the underside.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.treatment: Apply fungicides labeled for leaf mold; improve ventilation to reduce humidity.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Reduce humidity around plants; increase spacing between plants; use resistant varieties.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.description: A fungal disease caused by Septoria lycopersici.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small, circular spots with dark borders and tan/gray centers, often containing tiny black specks (pycnidia).
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.treatment: Apply fungicides at first sign of disease; remove and destroy infected leaves.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Rotate crops; mulch around plants; avoid overhead irrigation that splashes spores onto foliage.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.description: A pest infestation caused by Tetranychus urticae rather than a pathogen.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.symptoms: Fine yellow stippling/speckling on leaves; fine webbing on the underside; leaf bronzing and drop in severe cases.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.treatment: Apply miticides or insecticidal soap; introduce predatory mites for biological control.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Avoid drought-stressing plants; monitor regularly; avoid excess nitrogen fertilization.

Tomato___Target_Spot.description: A fungal disease caused by Corynespora cassiicola.
Tomato___Target_Spot.symptoms: Brown lesions with concentric rings on leaves, stems, and fruit, resembling a target.
Tomato___Target_Spot.treatment: Apply fungicides labeled for target spot; remove and destroy infected plant debris.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Rotate crops; ensure good airflow between plants; minimize prolonged leaf wetness.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.description: A viral disease transmitted primarily by whiteflies.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.symptoms: Upward curling and yellowing of leaves; stunted plant growth; reduced flowering and fruit drop.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.treatment: No cure exists; remove and destroy infected plants promptly; control the whitefly vector.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use resistant varieties; use reflective mulches or row covers; manage whitefly populations.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.description: A viral disease (ToMV) spread mechanically through contact, tools, and infected seed.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.symptoms: Mottled light and dark green mosaic pattern on leaves; leaf curling; stunted growth; fruit discoloration.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.treatment: No cure exists; remove and destroy infected plants; disinfect tools and hands after handling.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use certified virus-free seed; disinfect tools between plants; control weeds that can harbor the virus.

Tomato___healthy.description: No signs of disease were detected on this leaf.
Tomato___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper watering, staking, and routine monitoring for early signs of pests or disease.
